# Vila Vrioni

> A protected late Ottoman manor in Berat, Albania's UNESCO World Heritage city. Ancestral home of the Vrioni dynasty, restored by family descendants.

## About

Vila Vrioni is a heritage property in the Mangalem quarter of Berat, Albania, officially designated as a Protected Cultural Monument under the name "Objekti i Yzedin Galip Pashë Vrioni." The compound represents Albania's finest surviving example of late Ottoman residential architecture, featuring stone barrel vaults, hand-carved walnut coffered ceilings, an arched konak, and a limestone courtyard.

## The Vrioni Family

The Vrioni dynasty is one of Albania's most distinguished aristocratic families, spanning four centuries. Notable members include:

- Omer Pasha Vrioni I (d. 1828) — Ottoman general
- Mehmet Ali Pasha Vrioni (1842-1895) — Vice-President of the League of Prizren
- Iliaz Bey Vrioni (1882-1932) — Prime Minister of Albania, signatory of Independence
- Jusuf Vrioni (1916-2001) — Translator, Ambassador to UNESCO
- Izedin Vrioni — Mayor of Berat (5 terms), commander in the Vlora War (1920)

## The Property

- Location: Mangalem quarter, Berat, Albania (UNESCO World Heritage City)
- Type: Late Ottoman manor compound
- Status: Protected Cultural Monument, under active restoration since 2021
- Features: Stone barrel vaults, walnut coffered ceilings, arched konak, limestone courtyard, Ottoman hamami
- Owner: Izedin Vrioni (historical); restored by family descendants

## Contact

- Website: https://vrioni.al
- Enquiries: Private enquiries welcome via the website contact form
